title: Enemy Abilities System
description: Implementation reference for enemy special rule abilities triggered from enemy cards (Foundry VTT Dialog instances). Each ability has a clickable button on the enemy card portrait, targeting logic to apply effects, active effect indicators on target cards, real-time dialog updates via Foundry hooks, and cleanup when tokens are deleted. All code lives in module/tools-sheet.js inside the showEnemyRollDialog() function. Styling in css/tools.css.

systemContext.game: Monolith Conan board game (NOT Modiphius 2d20 Conan)
systemContext.enemyData: data/enemies.json contains all enemy profiles by category
systemContext.attackFormula: 1[StatDie] + StatValue + Modifiers vs Defense (Physical or Sorcery)
systemContext.damageFormula: Separate roll, e.g. 1d10+2. Not affected by attack modifiers.
systemContext.keyDistinction: Attack roll modifiers (like Inspire +1) only affect the hit check, never damage

architecture.overview: Enemy cards are Foundry Dialog instances created by showEnemyRollDialog() in module/tools-sheet.js (~line 5280+). They are NOT ActorSheet subclasses. The dialog receives enemyData from token flags (tokenDoc.flags.conan.enemyData) and renders a custom HTML layout with portrait, stats, weapons, and rules.

abilityButtons.generation: Built during content HTML generation before dialog creation (~line 5415). Each rule in enemyData.rules[] gets a button. State classes are set based on token flags at render time.

activeEffects.layout: flex-direction: column — Protect always first in DOM (top position), other effects stack below
activeEffects.generation: Built during content HTML generation (~line 5435). Checks actor flags for protectBonus and inspireBuff. Updated in real-time via updateActor hook.
activeEffects.principle: Use text labels (not icons) for effect names to avoid running out of icons. Exception: Protect uses shield icon + count badge since it stacks numerically.

targeting.method: Hooks.on('controlToken') captures when GM clicks a token on the canvas
targeting.prerequisite: canvas.tokens.releaseAll() MUST be called before registering the hook, otherwise clicking an already-selected token won't fire the hook
targeting.singleTarget: Protect: controlToken fires once, applies immediately, unhooks
targeting.multiTarget: Inspire: controlToken fires for each click, accumulates token IDs in array. Separate keyup listener on 'Shift' triggers activation.
targeting.cancel: Escape key or clicking the pulsing button again cancels targeting mode

realTimeHooks.setup: Registered inside the dialog's render callback (~line 5571). Two hook IDs tracked: updateHookId (updateActor) and tokenUpdateHookId (updateToken).
realTimeHooks.cleanup: Both hooks are unregistered in the dialog's close callback via Hooks.off().

updateActor.actions: Rebuilds active effects display, toggles protect-boosted class on defense, rebuilds status grid. Re-attaches click handlers to new DOM elements using specific selectors like .active-effect-btn[data-effect='protect'].

updateToken.actions: Toggles ability button classes (ability-active, ability-spent), updates defense value text.

realTimeHooks.gotcha: Foundry's unsetFlag creates a '-=flagName' key in the changes object, so both setFlag and unsetFlag changes must be detected.

deletionCleanup.hook: Hooks.on('deleteToken') — global hook registered at module level (~line 5167)
cases: Protected target deleted → iterate protectBonus.sources, clear protectUsed and protectTarget on each guard
Guard deleted → find protectTarget, remove this guard from target's protectBonus.sources, decrement total, revert enemyData.physicalDefense
Captain deleted → iterate inspireTargets, unsetFlag inspireBuff on each target actor
Inspired target deleted → find inspireBuff.source captain, remove this target from captain's inspireTargets. If no targets remain, set captain to 'spent' state
deletionCleanup.order: Protect cleanup runs first, then Inspire cleanup, then actor deletion (enemyData.actorId)

protect.ruleText: A Guard may use 1 Action to Protect a Character within Touch Range. For each Guard that Protects, the target gains +1 Physical Defense, up to a maximum of +3.

workflow.step1: GM clicks Protect button on guard's enemy card
workflow.step3: Button enters targeting state (pulsing animation, class: ability-targeting)
workflow.step4: controlToken hook registered — waits for GM to click a target token
workflow.step5: GM clicks target token on canvas
workflow.step6: Validation: not self, not already protected by this guard, target not at +3 cap
workflow.step7: Apply: setFlag protectBonus on target actor (increment total, add guard to sources), setFlag protectUsed/protectTarget on guard token, update enemyData.physicalDefense on target token
workflow.step8: Guard button goes green (ability-active). Target card shows shield+count. Defense value turns green.

dismissWorkflow.from_target_card: Click shield icon → removes most recent guard (sources.pop()), decrements total. If total reaches 0, unsetFlag protectBonus entirely.
dismissWorkflow.from_guard_card: Click green Protect button → looks up protectTarget, removes this guard from target's sources, decrements total, clears guard's protectUsed/protectTarget flags.

conan.protectUsed.description: Whether this guard has used Protect (true = active, shown as green button)

conan.protectTarget.description: Token ID of the target being protected. Used for dismiss-from-guard and deletion cleanup.

conan.protectBonus.description: Cumulative protection bonus. sources array tracks which guards are contributing. Max total of 3.
conan.protectBonus.cleanup: When total reaches 0, use unsetFlag (not setFlag with 0) to fully remove

conan.enemyData.physicalDefense.description: The displayed physical defense value on the enemy card. Incremented/decremented alongside protectBonus. Note: enemyData is the full enemy data object, so updates use spread: { ...existingData, physicalDefense: newValue }

active.appearance: Green border, green text, green hover glow

targetEffectDisplay.position: Always first (top) in the active effects column
targetEffectDisplay.dismiss: Each click decrements by 1 (removes most recent guard via sources.pop()). When count reaches 0, button and container removed.

inspire.ruleText: Once per combat, a Guard Captain may use 2 Actions to inspire their allies. All Characters within Long Range that are allied to the Guard Captain apply a +1 Modifier to their Attacks for the remainder of the current combat.

workflow.step1: GM clicks Inspire button on captain's enemy card
workflow.step3: Button enters targeting state (pulsing). controlToken hook and keyup listener registered.
workflow.step4: GM clicks targets one at a time — each click fires controlToken, token ID added to inspireSelected array. Notification shows count.
workflow.step5: GM presses Shift key — keyup handler fires, activates the ability
workflow.step6: Apply: setFlag inspireBuff on each target actor, setFlag inspireTargets and inspireState='active' on captain token
workflow.step7: Captain button goes green. Target cards show 'Inspire' text button. Chat message lists all targets.
workflow.step8_deactivate: Click green button → removes inspireBuff from all targets, sets inspireState='spent', button goes dark
workflow.step9_reset: Click dark button → unsets inspireState, button returns to ready for re-use

dismissWorkflow.from_target_card: Click 'Inspire' text button → unsetFlag inspireBuff on this target, remove from captain's inspireTargets. If no targets remain, captain goes to 'spent'.
dismissWorkflow.from_captain_card: Click green Inspire button → unsetFlag inspireBuff on ALL targets, captain goes to 'spent' state.

conan.inspireState.description: 3-state cycle: null → 'active' → 'spent' → null (clicking spent resets to ready)

conan.inspireTargets.description: Array of token IDs currently being inspired. Cleared when deactivated.

conan.inspireBuff.description: Tracks which captain is inspiring this target and the bonus value.

targetEffectDisplay.position: Below Protect in the active effects column (if both present)
targetEffectDisplay.dismiss: Click removes this target's inspireBuff and updates captain's target list

combatEffect.what: +1 to attack rolls (the hit check: 1[StatDie] + StatValue + Modifiers vs Defense)
combatEffect.implementation: In the weapon-btn click handler, check token.actor.getFlag('conan', 'inspireBuff'). If present, add 1 to skillTotal.
combatEffect.chatDisplay: Green +1 shown inline: 'Hit (Edge): d6: 4 +2 +1 = 7'
combatEffect.codeLocation: weapon-btn click handler in the dialog render callback (~line 5922)

inspire.tooltip: Click targets, then press Shift to activate

skulkers.ruleText: A Bandit applies a +1 Modifier to any Edge Checks related to hiding and stealth.
skulkers.description: Simple click-to-roll ability. No targeting, no state, no flags. Click the button, it rolls an Edge check with the +1 Skulkers bonus baked in.

workflow.step1: GM clicks Skulkers button on any bandit enemy card
workflow.step2: Immediately rolls Edge check: 1[EdgeDie] + EdgeValue + 1 (Skulkers bonus)
workflow.step3: Posts chat message with roll result and flavor quote

chatOutput.flavorQuote: "{enemyName} was born in the shadows and knows its secrets better than most."
chatOutput.greenBonus: The +1 Skulkers bonus is shown in green to distinguish it from the base stat

skulkers.codeLocation: ability-btn click handler, before the DEFAULT generic handler (~line 6252)

abilityTypes.instant-roll: Click button, immediately rolls dice and posts to chat. No targeting, no state. Examples: Skulkers.
abilityTypes.single-target: Click button, click one target, applies effect. Has source/target flags and real-time hooks. Examples: Protect.
abilityTypes.multi-target: Click button, click multiple targets, press Shift to activate. Has source/target flags, 3-state cycle, real-time hooks. Examples: Inspire.

classes..enemy-ability-buttons: Container: position absolute, bottom-left of portrait, flex column, gap 3px, z-index 5
classes..ability-btn: Small button: 11px font, theme border, 3px 6px padding, pointer cursor
classes..ability-btn.ability-targeting: Pulsing: theme-accent background, white text, ability-pulse animation 0.8s infinite alternate
classes..ability-btn.ability-active: Green: black bg, #32CD32 border/text, green hover glow
classes..ability-btn.ability-spent: Dark: black bg, #444 border, #666 text, 60% opacity
classes..enemy-active-effects: Container: position absolute, bottom-right of portrait, flex column, gap 3px, z-index 5
classes..active-effect-btn: Effect button: black bg, #32CD32 border/text, 12px font, flex row with gap for icon+count
classes..active-effect-btn:hover: Red: #8B0000 bg, #DC143C border, white text (dismiss indicator)
classes..def-value.protect-boosted: Green text with glow on physical defense value
classes.@keyframes ability-pulse: Box-shadow oscillates from 4px to 12px theme glow

codeLocations.showEnemyRollDialog: module/tools-sheet.js ~line 5280 — the main function that builds and shows enemy cards
codeLocations.abilityButtonGeneration: ~line 5415 — builds ability button HTML with state detection from token flags
codeLocations.activeEffectsGeneration: ~line 5435 — builds active effects HTML (protect shield+count, inspire text)
codeLocations.dialogHooksSetup: ~line 5571 — render callback registers updateActor and updateToken hooks
codeLocations.protectBonusRealtimeHandler: ~line 5680 in updateActor hook — rebuilds protect display, re-attaches handlers
codeLocations.inspireBuffRealtimeHandler: ~line 5733 in updateActor hook — rebuilds inspire display, re-attaches handlers
codeLocations.protectUsedRealtimeHandler: ~line 5770 in updateToken hook — toggles guard button green/normal
codeLocations.inspireStateRealtimeHandler: ~line 5832 in updateToken hook — toggles captain button green/spent/normal
codeLocations.weaponRollHandler: ~line 5922 — checks inspireBuff, adds +1 to attack roll and green indicator in chat
codeLocations.protectClickHandler: ~line 5968 in ability-btn click — full Protect targeting and application logic
codeLocations.inspireClickHandler: ~line 6052 in ability-btn click — full Inspire 3-state and multi-target logic
codeLocations.protectDismissHandler: ~line 6156 — active-effect-btn click handler for protect dismiss
codeLocations.inspireDismissHandler: ~line 6253 — active-effect-btn click handler for inspire dismiss
codeLocations.deleteTokenCleanup: ~line 5167 — global deleteToken hook handles all 4 cleanup cases

implementationPattern.description: Follow this pattern when adding a new enemy ability:
steps: 1. Define token flags for source state and target effect (see flags sections above)
2. Add state detection in ability button generation (~line 5415) — check token flags, set stateClass
3. Add active effect HTML in effects generation (~line 5435) — check target actor flags
4. Add click handler in ability-btn handler (~line 5968) — implement targeting, validation, application
5. Add dismiss handler in active-effect-btn handler (~line 6156) — handle removal from target card
6. Add real-time updateActor handler for target effect flag changes (~line 5680)
7. Add real-time updateToken handler for source state flag changes (~line 5770)
8. Add deleteToken cleanup cases (~line 5167) for both source-deleted and target-deleted
9. Add CSS classes for any new button states in css/tools.css
10. If the ability modifies rolls, update the weapon-btn click handler (~line 5922)
